title: Count active incidents with GlideAggregate
question: Write a background script that counts active incidents by priority using GlideAggregate.
summary: Uses GlideAggregate to count active incidents grouped by priority.
exactAnswer: Use GlideAggregate on the incident table, filter active records, group by priority, then print the count for each priority bucket.
steps: Open All > System Definition > Scripts - Background.
Review the output grouped by priority.
commonMistakes: Using GlideRecord and looping only to calculate a simple count.
Forgetting groupBy before reading grouped aggregates.
Reading a display value when the numeric value is intended.
explanation: GlideAggregate pushes the counting work to the database.
groupBy('priority') creates one row per priority value.
getAggregate('COUNT') returns the record count for that group.

title: Find stale incidents with an encoded query
question: Write a background script that finds active incidents not updated in the last 7 days using an encoded query.
summary: Shows how to use addEncodedQuery for a stale incident check.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecord with addEncodedQuery to filter active incidents whose Updated timestamp is older than 7 days, then print the incident numbers for review.
steps: Run it and review the output list.
commonMistakes: Using a malformed relative date expression.
Running the query without verifying timezone expectations.
Returning too many fields when only identifiers are needed.
explanation: Encoded queries are useful when the condition is easier to express in one string.
The relative date operator checks whether the record was updated before the last 7 days threshold.

title: Find possible duplicate CIs by serial number
question: Write a script to identify possible duplicate computer CIs sharing the same serial number.
summary: Uses GlideAggregate on cmdb_ci_computer to find duplicate serial numbers.
exactAnswer: Use GlideAggregate on cmdb_ci_computer, group by serial_number, add a HAVING condition for counts greater than 1, and print the duplicate serial values.
steps: Run the script and review duplicate serial values.
commonMistakes: Looking for duplicates without excluding blank serial numbers.
Grouping by the wrong class or table.
Assuming every duplicate serial is a true duplicate without checking the source context.
explanation: This script finds collision candidates, not the final remediation decision.
Use it as part of CMDB governance and IRE debugging.

title: Check pending ECC Queue output records
question: Write a script to list recent ECC Queue output records still waiting to be processed.
summary: Queries pending ECC output to help troubleshoot discovery and MID issues.
exactAnswer: Query the ecc_queue table for output records still in ready or processing states and print the topic, agent, and creation time for triage.
steps: Run the ECC Queue query script.
Review the output for stuck work items.
commonMistakes: Ignoring the queue field and mixing input with output.
Reviewing ECC records without correlating to MID health and discovery schedule context.
explanation: This script narrows the view to outbound work that may not yet be processed by the MID Server.

title: Check for change blackout conflicts
question: Write a script that flags normal changes scheduled during blackout windows.
summary: Illustrates a conflict-style validation for blackout maintenance schedules.
exactAnswer: Use GlideRecord to pull open normal changes with planned dates, then compare their windows against blackout schedule spans to identify conflicts for review.
steps: Run it and review conflicting changes.
expectedOutput: CHG0001234 overlaps blackout window Year End Freeze
commonMistakes: Comparing scheduled dates without checking both overlap boundaries.
Assuming every environment uses the same blackout schedule design.
explanation: This demonstrates overlap logic rather than a production-ready reusable utility class.

title: Find SAM reclamation candidates
question: Write a script to identify installed software candidates for reclamation based on inactive users.
summary: Queries software installations linked to inactive or retired users.
exactAnswer: Query software installations where the assigned user is inactive or retired, then print the software model and device details so the candidate set can be reviewed before reclamation.
steps: Review candidate rows before any automation is applied.
commonMistakes: Triggering reclamation from a raw query without human validation.
Ignoring shared devices or service accounts.
explanation: The script helps build a candidate list; policy and approval logic should govern final reclamation.

title: Validate HAM stockroom transfers
question: Write a script to find in-stock hardware assets missing a stockroom assignment.
summary: Supports asset data quality checks for stockroom-controlled inventory.
exactAnswer: Query in-stock hardware assets where the stockroom is empty and print the asset tag and model so inventory control gaps can be corrected.
steps: Run the hardware asset quality script.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong install status value for local environments.
Assuming all inventory gaps should be auto-corrected without operational review.
explanation: The goal is to expose stockroom control issues, not silently repair them in bulk.
